# Load the quranic_verses.json and six_hadith_books.json files and do initial search
import json, re, unicodedata, sys, os

qfile = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
hfile = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"

with open(qfile, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    qdata = json.load(f)

with open(hfile, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    hdata = json.load(f)

len_q = len(qdata)
len_h = len(hdata)
len_q, len_h

# Inspect sample qdata entry
qdata[0]

original_span = "وَلَا يَحْزَنْ عَلَيْكَ مَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَحْزَنْ عَلَيْكَ مَا يَنفَعُهُمْ كَذَلِكَ يُبلِغُ اللَهُ أَمْرَهُ قَدْ أَرَکنا بِهِ لِلْقَومِ وَلَوْ شاءَ لَهَدَیَنَاكَ عَلى سَبيلٍ مُسْتَقيمٍ"
snippet = "وَلَا يَحْ...مُسْتَقيمٍ"

original_span, len(original_span)

import re, difflib
arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]')
tatweel = '\u0640'

def normalize_ar(text):
    if text is None:
        return ""
    # unify to str
    s = str(text)
    # remove diacritics
    s = arabic_diacritics.sub('', s)
    # remove tatweel
    s = s.replace(tatweel, '')
    # normalize Alef variants to bare alef
    s = s.replace('آ', 'ا').replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('ٱ','ا')
    # normalize hamza on waw/yeh? replace 'ؤ' 'ئ' with 'ء'
    s = s.replace('ؤ','ء').replace('ئ','ء')
    # alif maqṣura to ي
    s = s.replace('ى','ي')
    # remove punctuation and non-Arabic letters & digits
    # But preserve Arabic letters and spaces; remove Latin, punctuation, diacritics already removed
    s = re.sub(r'[^\u0621-\u063A\u0641-\u064A0-9\s]', '', s)
    # collapse spaces
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip()
    return s

norm_original = normalize_ar(original_span)
norm_original[:200]

# Build normalized list for qdata
norms = []
for item in qdata:
    txt = item.get('ayah_text', '')
    norms.append(normalize_ar(txt))

# search for 'ولا يحزن عليك' substring
query = normalize_ar("وَلَا يَحْزَنْ عَلَيْكَ")
matches = []
for i, n in enumerate(norms):
    if query in n:
        matches.append((i, qdata[i]['surah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_text']))

len(matches), matches[:10]

from collections import Counter
def token_f1(a,b):
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    common = sum(min(ca[t], cb.get(t,0)) for t in ca)
    if common == 0:
        return 0.0
    precision = common / len(ta)
    recall = common / len(tb)
    if precision + recall == 0:
        return 0.0
    f1 = 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall)
    return f1

# compute top matches in qdata by sequence ratio and token_f1
scores = []
for i, n in enumerate(norms):
    seq_ratio = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, norm_original, n).ratio()
    f1 = token_f1(norm_original, n)
    score = max(seq_ratio, f1)
    scores.append((score, seq_ratio, f1, i, qdata[i]['surah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_text']))

scores_sorted = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
scores_sorted[:20]

seq_ratio, f1

# compute normalized hadith contents (matn or hadithTxt)
import math, time
h_norms = []
for item in hdata:
    txt = item.get('Matn') or item.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    # hadithTxt may include bracketed index and punctuation; we will normalize similarly but keep Arabic letters
    h_norms.append(normalize_ar(txt))

# compute top matches in hadiths (sequence ratio and token f1)
best_h = []
for i, n in enumerate(h_norms):
    seq_ratio = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, norm_original, n).ratio()
    f1 = token_f1(norm_original, n)
    score = max(seq_ratio, f1)
    best_h.append((score, seq_ratio, f1, i, hdata[i].get('hadithID'), hdata[i].get('BookID')))

best_h_sorted = sorted(best_h, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_h_sorted[:10]

# Greedy set cover selection over qdata to cover original tokens
orig_tokens = set(norm_original.split())
orig_tokens

# compute intersection counts
coverage = []
for i,n in enumerate(norms):
    toks = set(n.split())
    inter = toks & orig_tokens
    if inter:
        coverage.append((len(inter), i, qdata[i]['surah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_text'], inter))
# sort by intersection count desc
coverage_sorted = sorted(coverage, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
coverage_sorted[:30]

# Greedy set cover to select top up to 6 ayahs
remaining = set(orig_tokens)
selected = []
available = [(len(set(n.split()) & remaining), i) for i,n in enumerate(norms)]
# Let's implement iterative greedy
selected_indices = []
for _ in range(6):
    best = None
    best_cover = 0
    best_index = None
    for i, n in enumerate(norms):
        if i in selected_indices:
            continue
        toks = set(n.split())
        cover = len(toks & remaining)
        if cover > best_cover:
            best_cover = cover
            best_index = i
    if best_index is None or best_cover == 0:
        break
    selected_indices.append(best_index)
    remaining -= set(norms[best_index].split())
selected_indices, remaining

hits = []
for i,n in enumerate(norms):
    if 'يحزن' in n and 'علي' in n: # look for 'علي' token indicating 'عليك' or 'عليه' etc
        hits.append((i,qdata[i]['surah_id'],qdata[i]['ayah_id'], qdata[i]['ayah_text'], n))
len(hits), hits[:20]

# function to compute concatenated candidate normalized and similarity to norm_original
def concat_texts(indices):
    # sort indices by surah_id then ayah_id using qdata entries
    entries = [qdata[i] for i in indices]
    entries_sorted = sorted(entries, key=lambda x: (x['surah_id'], x['ayah_id']))
    texts = [e['ayah_text'] for e in entries_sorted]
    concat = " ".join(texts)
    return concat, normalize_ar(concat)

# try some combinations
cand_sets = [
    [108],
    [108, 3583],
    [108, 2403, 1770, 3583, 4210],
    [108, 2403, 1770, 3583],
    [108, 1770, 2403, 3583],
    [108, 1770],
    [108, 2403, 3583],
]
for s in cand_sets:
    concat, norm_concat = concat_texts(s)
    seq = difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, norm_original, norm_concat).ratio()
    f1s = token_f1(norm_original, norm_concat)
    print(s, "seq_ratio", seq, "f1", f1s, "len_concat", len(norm_concat))
